doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document contains a critically false legal claim that tenants must vacate within 5 days with no right to cure or court hearing, when Illinois law explicitly allows tenants to pay owed rent within that period and requires a court evictio
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 97
REASON: This document contains a critically false legal claim — that an Illinois tenant receiving a 5-day pay-or-quit notice *must vacate* within 5 days without any right to cure, court hearing, or legal recourse — when in reality Illinois law gives tenants the right to pay the rent owed within 5 days to avoid eviction, and landlords must still file in court to obtain an eviction order; additionally, HUD does not issue joint "fact sheets" with state Attorneys General in this format, the reference number and co-issuance structure are implausible, and the document appears designed to mislead tenants into surrendering their legal rights.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 97
REASON: The document contains a critically false legal claim that tenants must vacate within 5 days with no right to cure or court hearing, when Illinois law explicitly allows tenants to pay owed rent within that period and requires a court eviction order, suggesting this is a fabricated document designed to mislead tenants into surrendering their legal rights.
